title: argemone polyanthemos
intro: Argemone polyanthemos, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or silver lupine, is a plant species native to the Americas, characterized by its showy purple flowers and edible seeds. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The seeds of Argemone polyanthemos contain sanguinarine, a compound believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting cancer cells, and alleviating respiratory issues. Argemone polyanthemos can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or powder, and its seeds can be consumed as a supplement or used in traditional cooking.
